body:
## Summary
- Add a `paginate()` helper in `src/items.js`: default page size **20** (clamped to `1..100`), default offset **0** (clamped to `>=0`); non-integer/negative input falls back to defaults.
- Wire it into the `GET /items` handler (`src/server.js`), reading `limit`/`offset` query params and returning pagination metadata `{ items, total, limit, offset }`.
- Document the query params in the README and add unit tests (`test/items.test.js`, `node --test`).

**Behavior change (intentional):** `GET /items` with no params now returns the first 20 items instead of the full list, per the issue's acceptance criteria (default page size 20). Clients page via `limit`/`offset`; `total` always reflects the full count.

Closes #5. Also resolves the duplicate request in #1.

## Test plan
- `node --test` — 6 passing unit tests: default page size 20, offset skipping, limit cap at 100, non-numeric/negative fallback, string coercion.
- Manual: `GET /items` -> 20 items, `total: 57`; `GET /items?limit=5&offset=50` -> 5 items starting at id 51.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(items): add limit/offset pagination to /items
